headline: Extreme Heat Warnings Expand to US Southeast as Heat Index Hits 116°F — Colorado Wildfire Burn Scar Now Threatens Debris Flows
overview: A dangerous multi-hazard heat emergency engulfs large portions of the United States on July 8, with Extreme Heat Warnings now stretching from the Desert Southwest through the Carolinas and coastal Georgia, where heat indices are forecast to reach 112–116°F. In southern Colorado, the catastrophic Aspen Acres wildfire has spawned a secondary crisis as Flash Flood Watches warn of debris flows over the scorched burn scar. Meanwhile, widespread flooding persists across the Midwest and Great Plains, Southern European wildfires continue to rage under a prolonged heatwave, and Kīlauea volcano on Hawai'i shows renewed inflation signaling potential re-eruption.

events.title: Extreme Heat Warnings Surge Into US Southeast — Heat Index to 116°F in Carolinas and Georgia
events.location: Coastal Georgia, Southeast South Carolina, Southeast North Carolina, USA
events.summary: The National Weather Service issued Extreme Heat Warnings on July 8 for coastal southeast Georgia, southeast South Carolina, and southeast North Carolina, with heat index values forecast to reach 112–116°F — a significant geographic expansion of the heat emergency beyond the Southwest. NWS Charleston warned of heat indices between 112 and 116°F for the coastal Georgia and South Carolina zones, while NWS Wilmington issued warnings for portions of the Carolinas with indices up to 113°F. The combination of high temperatures and extreme humidity dramatically amplifies the life-threatening risk, particularly for outdoor workers and those without air conditioning.

events.title: Colorado Aspen Acres Wildfire Spawns Secondary Debris Flow Crisis — Flash Flood Watch Over Burn Scar
events.location: Southern Colorado (Wet Mountains, Pueblo County), USA
events.summary: The Aspen Acres Fire — already exceeding 127,000 acres with confirmed fatalities and dozens of structures destroyed — has generated a dangerous secondary hazard as NWS Pueblo issued a Flash Flood Watch for the burn scar area through Wednesday evening. Heavy rainfall falling on the denuded, hydrophobic soil of the burned landscape is expected to trigger rapid debris flows and flash flooding, threatening communities in Pueblo County and the Wet Mountains that were not directly impacted by the fire itself. This compound disaster scenario significantly complicates evacuation and recovery operations and places an entirely new population at imminent risk.

articles.title: Aspen Acres Fire continues to burn unchecked in southern Colorado
Satellite Images Show Colorado Fire As New Evacuations Ordered

events.title: US Southwest Extreme Heat Persists — Phoenix to 115°F, Warnings Extended Through Thursday
events.location: Phoenix Metro, Tucson, Coachella Valley, Yuma, Southwest Arizona and Southeast California, USA
events.summary: Extreme Heat Warnings remain in effect across the Desert Southwest through 8 PM MST/PDT Thursday, with afternoon temperatures forecast between 109°F and 117°F across portions of Arizona and southeast California. NWS Phoenix warned of conditions between 111–115°F for the greater Phoenix metro area and up to 117°F in the Yuma, Coachella Valley, and San Diego County desert zones, while NWS Tucson maintained warnings for Pima County and the Tohono O'odham Nation. The Grand Canyon's lower elevations remain under a separate Extreme Heat Warning through Saturday — one of the longest-duration heat alerts on record for that area — as the anomalous heat dome shows no signs of retreating.

articles.title: Los Angeles Heat Builds Ahead of Hot Spell Across US Southwest

events.title: Midwest and Great Plains Flooding Expands — South Dakota Flash Floods Strike Aberdeen Overnight
events.location: South Dakota, Minnesota, Iowa, Illinois, Indiana, USA
events.summary: Overnight thunderstorms on July 7–8 dumped 2–4 inches of rain on northeastern South Dakota, triggering Flash Flood Warnings across Brown, Day, Marshall, Codington, Grant, and Roberts counties, with vehicles stalled in the city of Aberdeen. Flood Watches simultaneously covered central and west-central Minnesota as slow-moving thunderstorms tracked east through the overnight hours, with localized rainfall of up to 4 inches possible. The Illinois River Flood Warning — covering counties from LaSalle south through Valley City and extended to at least July 12 — continues, while the Patoka River in Indiana is approaching record flood levels, illustrating the vast geographic scale of the ongoing Midwest flood crisis.

articles.title: Weekend storms inundate Northwest Indiana with water

events.title: Southern Europe Heatwave and Wildfires Continue — France, Greece, Portugal Under Siege
events.location: France, Greece, Portugal, Spain — Southern and Western Europe
events.summary: Western Europe's third major heatwave of summer 2026 continues to fuel dangerous wildfire conditions and strain power infrastructure across multiple countries, with thousands of residents evacuated from their homes in southwestern France, Greece, and Portugal. The Guardian reported that wildfires raging across southern Europe have forced mass evacuations, with Tour de France spectator bans enacted in fire-affected areas of France, while Insurance Journal noted that the heatwave is fueling 'powder keg' fire conditions across France, Spain, Portugal, and Greece. Weather data confirms Paris at 33.8°C and London at 31.6°C today, maintaining the dangerous anomaly driving wildfire risk.

articles.title: Wildfires rage across southern Europe, forcing thousands to flee homes
Europe Faces Weeks of Extreme Heat as Fire and Power Risks Build

events.title: Kīlauea Volcano Inflation Builds After Eruption Pause — Re-Eruption Possible
events.location: Kīlauea Summit, Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park, Hawaiʻi, USA
events.summary: The USGS Hawaiian Volcano Observatory reports that the ongoing eruption at Kīlauea's summit has entered a pause phase, with the Volcano Alert Level held at ADVISORY as ground inflation continues to build. Sustained inflation at the summit indicates that magma is accumulating in the shallow reservoir system beneath the caldera, a recognized precursor to renewed eruptive activity. USGS scientists are monitoring the situation closely, and a return to active lava effusion within the summit crater remains possible in the near term.

articles.title: Kīlauea Volcano Update for Sunday, July 5th

events.title: M3.8 Earthquake Near Oak Harbor, Washington — Over 2,000 Reports of Shaking
events.summary: A magnitude 3.8 earthquake struck 2 km east of Oak Harbor, Washington on July 2 at 06:35 UTC, at a depth of 25 km, generating an unusually high 2,027 felt reports submitted to the USGS — reflecting the dense population of Whidbey Island and surrounding Puget Sound communities. No tsunami alert was issued and no significant structural damage has been reported, consistent with the moderate magnitude, though the extensive felt reports underscore the earthquake's broad reach across the greater Seattle metropolitan area. USGS assigned no formal impact alert level, but the event stands as the most significant seismic activity recorded in the region in the past seven days.
